methods.description: Isolation of the diatom A. glacialis strain A3 along with its natural bacterial community for experimental analysis.

steps.description: The diatom A. glacialis strain A3 was isolated along with its natural bacterial community as previously described.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate A. glacialis strain A3 and its bacterial community
outputs: Isolated A. glacialis A3 and bacterial community

methods.description: Preparation of axenic cultures of A. glacialis A3 using antibiotics to remove bacteria.

steps.description: A. glacialis A3 cultures were made axenic using antibiotics and left to acclimate in the absence of bacteria.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare axenic cultures of A. glacialis A3 using antibiotics

methods.description: Reseeding of acclimated axenic A. glacialis A3 cultures with a bacterial consortium.

steps.description: To reseed the acclimated axenic A. glacialis A3 cultures, we used a consortium stock harvested from xenic A. glacialis A3 cultures after removing diatom cells.
steps.searchable_summary: reseed axenic A. glacialis A3 cultures with bacterial consortium

methods.description: Analysis of metagenomes and metatranscriptomes to understand bacterial responses and interactions with A. glacialis A3.

steps.description: Ten near-complete bacterial genomes were assembled from the consortium metagenome.
Metatranscriptomes from the consortium were used to uncover how different bacterial families responded to diatom exudates.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze metatranscriptomes for bacterial response to diatom exudates

methods.description: Analysis of exometabolites at different time points to understand metabolic interactions.

steps.description: Exometabolites were analyzed at four different time points with a Bruker Impact II HD quadrupole time-of-flight mass spectrometer (QToF-MS), and 28 molecules were confirmed against a library of standards.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze exometabolites using QToF-MS and confirm against standards

methods.description: Determination of the molecular composition of dissolved organic matter components using FT-ICR-MS.

steps.description: Fourier-transform ion cyclotron resonance mass spectrometry (FT-ICR-MS) was used to determine the molecular composition of dissolved organic matter components.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze molecular composition of dissolved organic matter using FT-ICR-MS

methods.description: Assays to confirm bacterial growth on diatom metabolites and assess motility.

steps.description: Isolated bacteria from the bacterial consortium and monitored their growth in supplemented media.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor bacterial growth in supplemented media

steps.description: Rosmarinic acid (2 μM) and azelaic acid (100 μM) were used to examine if diatom secondary metabolites can give Roseobacters an advantage over Alteromonadaceae by cell attachment with a bacterial motility assay or by modulation of bacterial growth, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: test effects of rosmarinic and azelaic acid on bacterial motility and growth

methods.description: Analysis of the global distribution and homology of an azelaic acid transcriptional regulator using metagenomics datasets.

steps.description: The global distribution and homology analysis of an azelaic acid transcriptional regulator was examined by querying a hidden Markov model profile against the Tara Oceans bacterial metagenomics datasets.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze global distribution and homology of transcriptional regulator using HMM and metagenomics datasets
